metadata.seed: Oh, there's a couple things. My classwork has been really been bogging me down lately, and then while I'm still doing better at work, I'm still not where I want to be. I don't want to have to consciously think this is why I'm. I want to keep working on. And I'm not being so argumentative at work. And then my boyfriend has been, like I said earlier, really gritting on my nerves, and I don't, like, I told him the other day that I wanted to break up with him, but he told me he was going to change and that things would get better. So I'm kind of torn with whether or not to continue in their relationship or just let it go. And at the same time, I'm having a hard time keeping up with my schoolwork and getting everything done on time. And my homework isn't where I want it to be. Like, I feel like I can do better.

patient.query: Oh, there's a couple things. My classwork has been really been bogging me down lately, and then while I'm still doing better at work, I'm still not where I want to be. I don't want to have to consciously think this is why I'm. I want to keep working on. And I'm not being so argumentative at work. And then my boyfriend has been, like I said earlier, really gritting on my nerves, and I don't, like, I told him the other day that I wanted to break up with him, but he told me he was going to change and that things would get better. So I'm kind of torn with whether or not to continue in their relationship or just let it go. And at the same time, I'm having a hard time keeping up with my schoolwork and getting everything done on time. And my homework isn't where I want it to be. Like, I feel like I can do better.

automatic_thoughts: I'm not where I want to be
I want to break up with him
He says he will change
I can do better

llm_response.response: It sounds like you're carrying a lot right now—balancing work, school, and your relationship, and feeling stuck between your own goals and what's happening around you. That mix of pressure and uncertainty can definitely make it hard to stay focused or feel in control.

validate_and_reflect.response: It sounds like you're carrying a lot right now—balancing work, school, and your relationship, and feeling stuck between your own goals and what's happening around you. That mix of pressure and uncertainty can definitely make it hard to stay focused or feel in control.

socratic_questioning.response: That's understandable. When he says he'll change, how does that belief impact your ability to feel hopeful or at peace about the relationship?

alternative_perspective.response: Thinking about what a trusted friend might say about this situation, what might they suggest as a different way to look at it?

safety_check.response: It sounds like you're carrying a lot right now—balancing school, work, and this relationship, and feeling stuck between your own needs and what your boyfriend is saying. Can you tell me more about how his response makes you feel?

patient.query: Yeah, it feels overwhelming sometimes. I keep trying to juggle everything, but it’s like I’m running in circles. I want to do well in school, but every time I sit down to study, my mind just... drifts. I guess I’m feeling a bit lost.

automatic_thoughts: I'm running in circles
I want to do well in school
my mind just drifts
I'm feeling a bit lost

llm_response.response: That sounds really challenging. When you say your mind drifts during study time, what thoughts come into your head that make it hard to stay focused?

validate_and_reflect.response: "It sounds like you're feeling really overwhelmed by everything right now, and it's causing you to feel lost. When you say you're running in circles, is that something that often happens when you're trying to focus on tasks like studying?"

socratic_questioning.response: That sounds really challenging. When you say your mind drifts during study time, what thoughts come into your head that make it hard to stay focused?

alternative_perspective.response:
FINAL RESPONSE:
It sounds like you're feeling stuck in a cycle, but I wonder if a trusted friend might see this differently? What do you think they might notice about your efforts?

safety_check.response: That sounds really challenging, and I want to make sure you're okay. When you said that, were you having any thoughts of hurting yourself or not wanting to be here?

patient.query: A lot of different things, I guess. Sometimes I worry about not understanding the material or falling behind. Other times, I think about how much pressure I put on myself to get good grades, and then I just... freeze up. It’s frustrating, because I know I can do the work, but I feel like I get in my own way.

automatic_thoughts: I put too much pressure on myself
I freeze up
I get in my own way

llm_response.response: That sounds really tough. When you say you freeze up, what thoughts are running through your mind right before that happens?

validate_and_reflect.response: It sounds like you're carrying a lot of pressure, especially around exams and keeping up with the material. When you mentioned freezing up, could you tell me more about what that feels like?

socratic_questioning.response: That sounds really tough. When you say you freeze up, what thoughts are running through your mind right before that happens?

alternative_perspective.response: It sounds like you're feeling caught between your goals and your anxiety about how you're doing. Can you tell me what you think a really supportive friend might say about this situation?

safety_check.response: It sounds like you're carrying a lot of pressure, especially around school and grades, which is causing you to worry and feel frustrated when things don't go smoothly. That must be really draining, especially when you feel like you're freezing up because of it.

retrieved_concepts_used.concept: I get in my own way

patient.query: It’s like this wave of “What if I fail?” or “What if I can’t keep up?” It just feels so heavy, and then I start to doubt myself more. I think about how disappointed others would be if I didn’t do well, and it paralyzes me. It’s like I want to run away from it all instead of facing it.

automatic_thoughts: What if I fail?
What if I can't keep up?
Others would be disappointed if I didn't do well

llm_response.response: It sounds like those heavy doubts about failing or disappointing others are really weighing you down, making it hard to move forward. That feeling of paralysis and wanting to run away must be incredibly draining for you.

validate_and_reflect.response: It sounds like those heavy doubts about failing or disappointing others are really weighing you down, making it hard to move forward. That feeling of paralysis and wanting to run away must be incredibly draining for you.

patient.query: Yeah, it really is. Sometimes I wish I could just ignore it all and pretend everything is fine. But then I get this nagging feeling in the back of my mind, like I’m letting people down or wasting my potential. It just spirals, you know? And I end up feeling even more stuck.

automatic_thoughts: I wish I could just ignore it all
I'm letting people down
I'm wasting my potential

entailment: Fear of being a burden to others (finding)

patient.query: Yeah, it really does feel like a tightrope walk sometimes. I want to succeed and make everyone proud, but then I just get so anxious about even starting. It’s like I’m so focused on not disappointing anyone that I forget to take care of myself in the process. It’s confusing.

automatic_thoughts: I will disappoint everyone
I can't start
I need to make everyone proud

neutral: Anxiety about saying the wrong thing (finding)

safety_check.response: It sounds really tough to balance all those expectations on yourself while feeling anxious about even taking the first step. Can you tell me more about how that internal conflict feels for you?
